details.text: Derivative: تَمِيدَ from مَادَ which means to move and shake. The meaning in respect to the رَوَاسِيَ (firmly anchored mountains) is that they do not move whereby they would cause you harm. Or that they prevent the earth from shaking you (the inhabitants of the earth) i.e., they provide stability to the earth.

Derivative: اشْكُرْ is in the command form (فِعْل الأَمْر) in the sentence وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا لُقْمَانَ الْحِكْمَةَ أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ instead of يَشْكُرَ. It changes from the third person to the first person. Instead of meaning: “We provided Luqman with the wisdom to be grateful to Allah” the meaning changes to: “We provided Luqman with the wisdom that: “Be grateful to Allah”” which provides emphasis and a sense of urgency. It implies the command to be grateful to Allah and for that to be a permanent and continuous state of the person.

Derivative: حَمِيدٌ means to be praised. The difference between حَمِيدٌ and مَحْمود (to be praised or praiseworthy) is that مَحْمود relies upon someone else doing the praising whilst حَمِيدٌ is not reliant on another party but rather means to be praised in and of Himself i.e., the praise is intrinsically attached to Allah, is independent and non-reliant.

- بُنَيَّ is the تَصْغِير form turning son into “young or little” son and is used as a term of endearment, love, care, warmth and affection in the phrase يَا بُنَيَّ i.e., O my dear beloved young son.
Derivative: وَهْن - to become weak, enfeebled, lose strength and to languish. This refers to the hardships that the mother faces during pregnancy and then childbirth.
Derivative: صَاحِبْ from صَاحَبَ – مُصاحَبَة and it means to maintain close and constant company.

Derivative: لَطِيفٌ can be translated to be subtle and comes from لُطْف (Lutf) which is a word that is difficult to translate using simple language. It reflects subtlety, grace, precision and delicateness.
- عَزْمِ الْأُمُورِ Are those matters or deeds that require great resolve and concern. Or the highest deeds and accomplishments.
Derivative: فَخُورٍ refers to someone who has extreme pride in himself.
Derivative: مَرَحًا to be happy, pleased and full of oneself i.e., in a conceited, posing and showing off manner.
Derivative: مُخْتَالٍ – It refers to someone who is so full of himself and flaunts his virtues or achievements.

means to turn the cheek. it is an expression for showing contempt at something or someone, similar to the english expression of turning one’s nose up at someone or something. it refers to the use of facial expressions to show contempt, the meaning of which is clear even though no words have been said.
- الْعُرْوَةِ is something strong that does not break. The one who grasps or holds onto it will be saved. الْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ means the strongest or surest handhold.

- بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ means what the breasts or chests contain inside them and is an expression similar to the conscience of the human being i.e., the inner truth of someone, his motivations and intentions.

Derivative: غَلِيظٍ is a description of something very tough that is inflexible and does not bend. A punishment (عَذَابٍ) which is غَلِيظٍ is one that is tough, severe and also unrelenting and uncompromising from which there is no escape.
our lives in this life are subservient to the schedule of the system that allah has subdued and subjected to follow its course i.e., the night and day, the sun and the moon.
- أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى is an expression which means a declared or well-named deadline or expiry date/term.

Derivative: صَبَّارٍ is the Mubaalaghah (exaggeration form فَعَّال) and refers to someone who shows patience and perseverance over and over again and in situation after situation i.e., continuously and consistently.
Derivative: شَكُورٍ is the فَعُول form which means to go out of one’s way to be grateful – someone who is extremely grateful.
Derivative: ٱلظُّلَل refers to clouds that are huge like mountains which cover what lies underneath it in shade.
Derivative: خَتَّارٍ refers to someone who betrays, is disloyal, treacherous and cheats or deceives (others) in the worst manner.

Derivative: مُّقْتَصِدٌ means to adopt a middle and balanced position but in this context it refers to a person taking the same position as he did prior to having being saved by Allah i.e., instead of turning to Allah in gratitude for saving him, he goes back to how he had been prior to be in danger.
Derivative: كَفُورٍ means extremely ungrateful.

Derivative: تَغُرَّنَّ and يَغُرَّنَّ from غَرَّ and it means to deceive, beguile or mislead. غُرُور (Ghuroor) is to take advantage of someone’s lack of awareness or goodwill and the one who does that is called غَرُور (Gharoor).

Derivative: ٱلسَّاعَةِ means the hour and refers to the final hour. An hour implies more urgency than a day and ٱلسَّاعَةِ in Arabic can also refer to a moment which is an even shorter period of time.
Derivative: ٱلْغَيْثَ is the rain that comes at its time of need i.e., the rain that provides relief.

- لَهْوَ is anything that diverts, distracts and amuses. لَهْوَ الْحَدِيثِ is the speech that provides amusement, distraction and acts as a diversion. The لَهْوَ الْحَدِيثِ which is purchased in this verse can be compared to the western entertainment industry in the current age which is heavily invested in, and which also includes a great deal of immorality and vulgarities. It creates a culture of misguidance.
